binaries.title: mssecflt.sys (Microsoft Security Filter minifilter driver) - use-after-free during process-context enumeration under shared lock

changed_functions.name: SecEnumerateProcessContexts
key_changes: UNPATCHED: iterates the 128-bucket process-context hash table (SecProcessTable, buckets at [SecProcessTable+0x140]) while holding only a SHARED push lock (FltAcquirePushLockSharedEx on SecProcessTable+8) and invokes a CFG-dispatched per-entry callback (call cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr on the pointer in rax) with the entry (node-0x10) as arg1, without taking a reference on the entry across the callback

PATCHED: adds a path selected when SecData+0x510 bit 0 is CLEAR (the default). On this path it walks the table under the shared lock, calls SecReferenceProcessContext on every entry to pin it, stores the pinned entries into a pool buffer sized from the entry count at [SecProcessTable+0x148] (ExAllocatePoolWithTag, tag 'Scce' = 65636353h), releases the shared lock, and only then invokes the callback on each pinned entry, calling SecReleaseProcessContext after each, then SecFreePool
PATCHED: the old in-lock callback traversal is retained and runs only when SecData+0x510 bit 0 is SET (test byte ptr cs:xmmword_14001B740+8, 1 ; jz snapshot path)
PATCHED: speculation-barrier (lfence) instructions appear on the new feature-gated path before the count-sized allocation and buffer use
changed_functions.security_notes: The unpatched enumerator invokes per-process policy callbacks (e.g., SecApplyReadWriteTelemetryPolicyForProcess, SecApplyFileOpenMonitoringForProcess, SecApplyTiLocalExecProtectVmForProcess) on each hash-table entry while holding only a shared push lock, and holds a raw pointer to the entry (node-0x10) and its next pointer across the call without pinning the entry. If the callback drops the entry's last reference or otherwise triggers its teardown (or a concurrent SecCleanupProcessContexts path releases it), the entry can be freed or recycled to the process-context lookaside list while the enumerator continues to use it, yielding a use-after-free. The patch fixes this by pinning every entry with SecReferenceProcessContext, snapshotting the pinned pointers, releasing the shared lock, and only then invoking callbacks (releasing the reference afterward). The gate direction is such that the safe snapshot path is the default (bit clear) and the old in-lock path is a registry-controlled fallback (bit set).

changed_functions.name: SecPerformProcessAssertionsForAllProcesses
key_changes: UNPATCHED: iterates the same process-context table under the shared SecProcessTable push lock and, for each entry, calls PsLookupProcessByProcessId on the entry's process id ([entry+0x20]), then SecDetPerformProcessAssertionsWithContext, then ObfDereferenceObject, without pinning the entry across the work

PATCHED: adds the same SecData+0x510 bit-0 check; when the bit is CLEAR (default) it delegates to SecEnumerateProcessContexts (the reference-and-snapshot path) instead of traversing under the lock; the old in-lock traversal is retained only when the bit is SET
Same use-after-free/race class as SecEnumerateProcessContexts, driven from timer maintenance rather than a direct request
changed_functions.security_notes: Same class as SecEnumerateProcessContexts. Called from the timer-driven maintenance routine SecDetTimerPerformDeferredAssertionsImpl (0x140042400). Process creation/termination populates and mutates the table concurrently, so the race is driven by process-lifetime activity. The patch routes the default path through the safe reference-and-snapshot enumerator.

changed_functions.name: SecCleanupProcessContexts
key_changes: PATCHED: decrements the entry count at [SecProcessTable+0x148] when removing entries, keeping the count accurate
This count sizes the snapshot allocation in the patched SecEnumerateProcessContexts
changed_functions.security_notes: Supporting change for the snapshot mechanism: maintains the entry count used for allocation sizing.

changed_functions.name: SecCreateProcessContext
key_changes: PATCHED: increments the entry count at [SecProcessTable+0x148] when inserting entries
Insertion feature-flag test updated (bit 2 -> bit 4)
changed_functions.security_notes: Supporting change for the snapshot mechanism: maintains the entry count used for allocation sizing.

changed_functions.name: sub_140029EF0
key_changes: PATCHED: extends the feature-flag block copied into SecData+0x508 from a single 8-byte value to two 8-byte values (16 bytes), zero-initialized then optionally loaded from the BootFeatureFlags registry binary value
The second qword holds the SecData+0x510 bit-0 path selector now read by the patched enumerators
changed_functions.security_notes: Supporting change: makes the second feature-flag qword available so the enumeration path selector can be read. The block defaults to zero (safe snapshot path) when the registry value is absent.

changed_functions.name: sub_14003BC14
key_changes: PATCHED: adds a SeGetCachedSigningLevel fast path gated by a feature bit in xmmword_14001B740
Adds a speculation barrier before the second SeGetCachedSigningLevel call
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-staged signing-level query change; old path retained. Not a security fix.

changed_functions.name: sub_14003BA6C
key_changes: PATCHED: adds a SecData+0x510 bit-2 gated alternate path that checks signing level via sub_14003befc (SeGetCachedSigningLevel) before proceeding
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-staged signing-level check; old path retained. Not a security fix.

changed_functions.name: sub_14002F788
key_changes: PATCHED: adds a SecData+0x510 bit-4 gated early-exit path
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-flag gating; not a security fix.

changed_functions.name: sub_140032D20
key_changes: PATCHED: dispatch-table handlers now capture return values (previously void calls)
Callee addresses shifted due to recompilation
changed_functions.security_notes: Return-value capture in dispatch handlers.

changed_functions.name: sub_14004054C
key_changes: PATCHED: widens a feature-flag early-exit test from (!(x & 1)) to (x & 1 || x & 2)
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-flag gating for a process-information query.

changed_functions.name: sub_14003F6CC
key_changes: PATCHED: adds a bit-1 feature check alongside the existing bit-0 check
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-flag gating.

changed_functions.name: sub_14003F9DC
key_changes: PATCHED: adds a bit-1 feature check alongside the existing bit-0 check
Captures the argument into a register at entry and uses it throughout
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-flag gating and argument capture.

changed_functions.name: sub_14003EBD8
key_changes: PATCHED: adds a feature-gated early-return condition (sub_14003f154 check)
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-flag gating.

changed_functions.name: sub_140024910
key_changes: Reorders IoStatus.Status/Information assignments and callback argument passing; register allocation differences
changed_functions.security_notes: Instruction reordering; no behavioral change.

changed_functions.name: sub_1400393F8
key_changes: Version string changes from 0x3f to 0x41
Global data addresses shift due to recompilation
changed_functions.security_notes: Version bump and address relocation.

changed_functions.name: sub_14003C294
key_changes: Reorders feature-flag bit assignments in the flags initialization word; adds one flag bit
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-flag reordering; no security impact.

changed_functions.name: SecMessage
key_changes: Minor recompilation differences in the communication-port message handler
changed_functions.security_notes: Mostly cosmetic changes in the message handler.

changed_functions.name: SecSetDriverConfiguration
key_changes: Global and callee addresses shift due to layout differences
Calls to SecEnumerateProcessContexts now target the patched function; core policy logic unchanged
changed_functions.security_notes: Address relocation and updated call target.

changed_functions.name: sub_14000AC40
key_changes: Minor recompilation differences

changed_functions.name: sub_14002CEA4
key_changes: Minor recompilation differences

changed_functions.name: sub_140039F98
key_changes: Feature-word store to xmmword_14001B740 widened to a 128-bit write; global addresses shift
changed_functions.security_notes: Initialization change; address relocation.

changed_functions.name: SecDetTimerPerformDeferredAssertionsImpl
key_changes: Call target updated to the patched SecPerformProcessAssertionsForAllProcesses (0x14002df1c)
changed_functions.security_notes: Timer-driven maintenance function with updated callee address.

changed_functions.name: j_sub_14002f788
key_changes: Jump thunk with updated addresses

changed_functions.name: sub_140029CE0
key_changes: Minor recompilation differences

security_findings.entry_point: Process-context enumeration invoked from policy/configuration apply (SecSetDriverConfiguration), process create/exit notifications, and timer maintenance. The configuration path is reachable through the \MicrosoftSecFilterControlPort minifilter communication port via SecMessage, but sending policy-configuration messages to that port normally requires the privileged management service.
call_chain: SecSetDriverConfiguration (applies policy; calls SecEnumerateProcessContexts with a per-process callback)
SecEnumerateProcessContexts (unpatched: invokes the callback on each entry while holding only a shared push lock, without pinning the entry)
security_findings.description: SecEnumerateProcessContexts iterates the 128-bucket process-context hash table (SecProcessTable, buckets at [SecProcessTable+0x140]) while holding only a SHARED push lock (FltAcquirePushLockSharedEx on SecProcessTable+8). For each list node it takes the entry object at node-0x10, advances the next pointer, and invokes a CFG-dispatched callback (call cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr on the pointer in rax) with the entry as arg1 and a caller context as arg2. No reference is held on the entry across the callback. The callbacks passed by SecSetDriverConfiguration are per-process policy handlers (SecApplyReadWriteTelemetryPolicyForProcess, SecApplyFileOpenMonitoringForProcess, SecApplyTiLocalExecProtectVmForProcess). If a callback drops the entry's last reference or triggers its teardown, or a concurrent SecCleanupProcessContexts path releases it, the entry is freed or recycled to the process-context lookaside list while the enumerator still holds a raw pointer to it and its next pointer, producing a use-after-free. The patch adds a default path (selected when SecData+0x510 bit 0 is clear) that pins each entry with SecReferenceProcessContext, snapshots the pinned pointers into a pool buffer (tag 'Scce') sized from the entry count at [SecProcessTable+0x148], releases the shared lock, and only then invokes callbacks, releasing each reference afterward and freeing the buffer. The old in-lock traversal is retained and runs only when the bit is set.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. The process-context table has entries (populated via SecCreateProcessContext as processes are tracked). 2. An enumeration is in progress (policy/configuration apply, process create/exit notification, or timer maintenance) that invokes callbacks on each entry. 3. Concurrently, the currently-enumerated entry has its last reference dropped, by the callback itself or by a concurrent teardown (SecCleanupProcessContexts), freeing or recycling it while the enumerator still holds a raw pointer. 4. The freed/recycled entry or its stale next pointer is then used.
security_findings.primitive: Kernel-pool use-after-free of a reference-counted process-context object during shared-lock enumeration. The object is recycled through the process-context lookaside list at SecProcessTable+0xC0. Loss of kernel memory safety during enumeration; the observable effect is a kernel bugcheck (denial of service). Reclaiming the freed allocation with controlled contents and any resulting control-flow or write primitive are not demonstrated by the diff and are not claimed.
security_findings.assembly:
; UNPATCHED SecEnumerateProcessContexts @ 0x14002CF04
0x14002CF29: mov     r8d, [rax+4F8h]
0x14002CF30: test    r8b, 4                     ; initialization flag
0x14002CF34: jz      0x14002CFAF
0x14002CF3F: add     rcx, 8
0x14002CF43: call    FltAcquirePushLockSharedEx ; SHARED lock — window opens
0x14002CF53: mov     ebp, 80h                   ; 128 buckets
0x14002CF58: mov     r8, [rax+140h]             ; bucket array base
0x14002CF5F: mov     rsi, [rbx+r8]              ; node = bucket head
0x14002CF65: lea     rcx, [rsi-10h]             ; entry = node-0x10 (arg1)
0x14002CF69: mov     rdx, r14                   ; context (arg2)
0x14002CF6C: mov     rsi, [rsi]                 ; advance node = node->next
0x14002CF6F: mov     rax, r15                   ; callback pointer
0x14002CF72: call    cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr ; callback(entry, context) UNDER SHARED LOCK
0x14002CF7F: mov     r8, [rax+140h]
0x14002CF8A: cmp     rsi, rax
0x14002CF8D: jnz     0x14002CF65
0x14002CF9B: jnz     0x14002CF5F                ; next bucket
0x14002CFAA: call    FltReleasePushLockEx       ; window closes
;
; PATCHED SecEnumerateProcessContexts @ 0x14002CFC0 — snapshot path (default)
0x14002D013: test    byte ptr cs:xmmword_14001B740+8, 1 ; SecData+0x510 bit 0
0x14002D01A: jz      0x14002D086               ; bit CLEAR -> snapshot path (default)
0x14002D08D: movsxd  rax, dword ptr [rcx+148h] ; entry count
0x14002D0C9: call    cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag ; tag 'Scce'
0x14002D121: call    SecReferenceProcessContext ; pin each entry under lock
0x14002D12C: mov     [rsi+rax*8], rbx          ; store pinned entry
0x14002D166: call    FltReleasePushLockEx      ; release BEFORE callbacks
0x14002D189: call    cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr ; callback on pinned entry
0x14002D192: call    SecReleaseProcessContext  ; release reference
0x14002D1A9: call    SecFreePool               ; free snapshot buffer

breakpoints.why: FltAcquirePushLockSharedEx — start of the shared-lock enumeration window.
mov rsi,[rsi] — the next-pointer read; if rsi refers to freed/recycled pool the traversal is following a stale node.
CFG-dispatched callback on the entry at rcx = node-0x10; the entry is not reference-pinned here.
Unlinks and releases process-context entries; if it drops the last reference on the enumerated entry concurrently, the enumerator's pointer becomes stale.

watch: r15 (callback pointer) and r14 (callback context) captured at entry
rsi at 0x14002cf65 = current list node; rcx at 0x14002cf72 = entry object (node-0x10)
[SecData+0x4f8] bit 2 = initialization flag gating the traversal
patched only: [SecProcessTable+0x148] = entry count for snapshot sizing; [SecData+0x510] bit 0 = path selector (clear = snapshot, set = old path)
key_offsets: 0x14002cf43 — FltAcquirePushLockSharedEx: start of the shared-lock window
0x14002cf5f — outer loop head: mov rsi,[rbx+r8] loads bucket head
0x14002cf65 — inner loop: lea rcx,[rsi-10h] prepares entry for callback
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Populate the process-context table by tracking processes (SecCreateProcessContext). Drive an enumeration via policy/configuration apply, process create/exit, or timer maintenance so callbacks run on each entry. Arrange for the enumerated entry's last reference to be dropped concurrently (callback-induced teardown or SecCleanupProcessContexts) so it is freed/recycled while the enumerator still holds a raw pointer.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: A dereference of freed or recycled pool during enumeration, typically surfacing as a kernel bugcheck (denial of service). A controllable memory-corruption primitive is not demonstrated.
debugging_aids.notes: The process-context table has 128 buckets (0x80) at [SecProcessTable+0x140]; entry objects sit at node-0x10. Entries are reference-counted (SecReferenceProcessContext / SecReleaseProcessContext) and recycled through the lookaside list at SecProcessTable+0xC0. The patched snapshot buffer uses tag 'Scce' (65636353h) and is sized from the count at [SecProcessTable+0x148]. The path selector at [SecData+0x510] bit 0 is a cached copy of the feature block loaded into xmmword_14001B740 from SecData+0x508; that block is zero-initialized and optionally overwritten from the BootFeatureFlags registry binary value, so the safe snapshot path is the default.

security_findings.entry_point: Timer-driven periodic maintenance (SecDetTimerPerformDeferredAssertionsImpl, 0x140042400). Autonomous; not a direct user-mode request.
call_chain: SecPerformProcessAssertionsForAllProcesses (unpatched: iterates the table under a shared lock, calls PsLookupProcessByProcessId + SecDetPerformProcessAssertionsWithContext on each entry without pinning it)
security_findings.description: Same class as SecEnumerateProcessContexts. This function iterates the process-context table under a shared push lock and, for each entry, calls PsLookupProcessByProcessId on the entry's process id ([entry+0x20]), then SecDetPerformProcessAssertionsWithContext, then ObfDereferenceObject, holding a raw pointer to the entry (node-0x10) across the work without pinning it. If a concurrent path frees or recycles the entry during the work, the enumerator uses stale memory. The patch adds the SecData+0x510 bit-0 check; when the bit is clear (default) it delegates to the safe reference-and-snapshot SecEnumerateProcessContexts, retaining the old in-lock traversal only when the bit is set.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Timer-driven, so periodic firing races against process creation/exit that add and remove entries via SecCreateProcessContext / SecCleanupProcessContexts. Rapid process churn increases the chance of the entry being freed while the timer traversal is using it.
security_findings.primitive: Kernel-pool use-after-free of a process-context object during timer-driven enumeration; observable as a kernel bugcheck. No controllable primitive is demonstrated.
security_findings.assembly:
; PATCHED SecPerformProcessAssertionsForAllProcesses @ 0x14002DF1C — gate + delegate
0x14002DF44: test    byte ptr cs:xmmword_14001B740+8, 1 ; SecData+0x510 bit 0
0x14002DF53: jnz     0x14002DF61                ; bit SET -> old in-lock traversal
0x14002DF57: call    SecEnumerateProcessContexts ; bit CLEAR (default) -> safe snapshot path
; old in-lock path (bit SET):
0x14002DF6E: call    FltAcquirePushLockSharedEx
0x14002DF96: lea     r14, [rsi-10h]            ; entry = node-0x10
0x14002DF9A: mov     rsi, [rsi]                ; advance
0x14002DFA6: call    cs:__imp_PsLookupProcessByProcessId
0x14002DFC1: call    SecDetPerformProcessAssertionsWithContext ; entry not pinned
0x14002DFCB: call    cs:__imp_ObfDereferenceObject
0x14002E001: call    FltReleasePushLockEx

breakpoints.why: Function entry for the timer-driven shared-lock traversal; observe the shared-lock acquisition and per-entry work.

watch: rsi = current list node during traversal
r14 = entry object (node-0x10) passed to PsLookupProcessByProcessId; watch for freed/recycled pool
[SecData+0x510] bit 0 = path selector (clear delegates to the safe enumerator)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Timer-driven: create and terminate processes rapidly so entries are added/removed while the periodic timer fires; no direct user-mode call is needed.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: A dereference of freed or recycled pool when the traversal uses an entry that a concurrent process-notification path freed; typically a kernel bugcheck.
debugging_aids.notes: The patch applies the same fix by delegating the default path to the reference-and-snapshot SecEnumerateProcessContexts (SecData+0x510 bit 0 gate).

summary: The patch adds a reference-and-snapshot enumeration path to SecEnumerateProcessContexts (unpatched 0x14002CF04, patched 0x14002CFC0) in the Microsoft Security Filter minifilter driver. The unpatched enumerator iterates the 128-bucket process-context hash table while holding only a shared push lock and invokes a per-entry callback with the entry (node-0x10) as argument, without holding a reference on the entry across the call. If a callback drops the entry's last reference or a concurrent teardown (SecCleanupProcessContexts) frees it, the entry is freed or recycled while the enumerator still uses it and its next pointer, a use-after-free. The patched default path pins each entry with SecReferenceProcessContext, snapshots the pinned pointers into a pool buffer (tag 'Scce') sized from the entry count at [SecProcessTable+0x148], releases the shared lock, and only then invokes callbacks (releasing each reference afterward). The path is selected when SecData+0x510 bit 0 is clear, which is the default (the feature block is zero-initialized and optionally loaded from a registry binary value); the old in-lock path is retained as a registry-controlled fallback (bit set). SecPerformProcessAssertionsForAllProcesses (0x14002DD1C -> 0x14002DF1C) shares the pattern and, on the default path, delegates to the safe enumerator. Impact is a kernel-mode use-after-free/race surfacing as a denial of service; a controllable memory-corruption primitive is not demonstrated, and the policy-configuration entry point via \MicrosoftSecFilterControlPort normally requires the privileged management service.
